report_metadata.cancer_type: Hodgkin Lymphoma, Cervical Stage IIA (Classical, Nodular Sclerosis)

treatments.name: 2x ABVD + 20 Gy Involved-Site RT (GHSG HD10 Protocol)

evidence_level.rationale: GHSG HD10 Phase 3 RCT with 1370 patients; international standard of care for early-stage favorable HL; long-term follow-up (median 79-91 months) confirms excellent outcomes

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 96.6%, 5-year PFS 91.2%; highly curable disease; cure rate >90% with combined modality therapy; minimal difference from more intensive 4x ABVD approach

accessibility.rationale: ABVD universally available worldwide; 20 Gy ISRT widely deliverable; WHO essential medicines; lowest cost frontline option; only 2 cycles minimizes treatment burden

safety_profile.rationale: Only 2 cycles of ABVD limits cumulative toxicity; 20 Gy ISRT has minimal late effects (2.9% vs 8.7% at 30 Gy); ABVD has lowest gonadotoxicity of HL regimens; no bleomycin-free option but 2 cycles limit pulmonary risk

biomarker_match.rationale: Standard approach for all classical HL subtypes including nodular sclerosis; CD30 expression not required for ABVD efficacy but confirms classical HL diagnosis

treatments.mechanism_of_action: ABVD: Doxorubicin (DNA intercalator/topoisomerase II inhibitor), Bleomycin (DNA strand breaks via free radicals), Vinblastine (microtubule inhibitor), Dacarbazine (alkylating agent). Combined with 20 Gy involved-site radiation therapy to consolidate local disease control. Two cycles deliver sufficient chemotherapy intensity for favorable-risk disease.
notable_side_effects: Bleomycin pulmonary toxicity (low risk with 2 cycles)

treatments.name: BV-AVD (Brentuximab Vedotin + AVD) — ECHELON-1 Regimen

evidence_level.rationale: ECHELON-1 Phase 3 RCT with 1334 patients; 7-year OS update available; FDA-approved first-line for Stage III/IV; increasingly used in unfavorable early-stage (BREACH trial)

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year PFS 82.2% vs 75.3% for ABVD in advanced stage; 6-year OS 93.9% vs 89.4%; significant OS benefit demonstrated; BREACH trial shows 97% 2-year PFS in early-stage unfavorable

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for advanced-stage HL; brentuximab vedotin widely available at cancer centers; higher cost than ABVD; European approval for Stage IIb+/III/IV with BrECADD

safety_profile.rationale: No bleomycin eliminates pulmonary toxicity risk; increased peripheral neuropathy (19% ongoing at 5 years vs 9% ABVD); febrile neutropenia higher (requires G-CSF prophylaxis)

biomarker_match.rationale: CD30-targeted ADC directly targets Reed-Sternberg cells; nodular sclerosis HL universally expresses CD30; molecular precision superior to untargeted ABVD

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Brentuximab vedotin is a CD30-directed antibody-drug conjugate delivering monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE) directly to CD30+ Reed-Sternberg cells, causing microtubule disruption and apoptosis. Combined with doxorubicin, vinblastine, and dacarbazine (AVD). Bleomycin omitted to eliminate pulmonary toxicity.

treatments.name: Nivolumab + AVD (N-AVD) — SWOG S1826 Regimen

evidence_level.rationale: SWOG S1826 Phase 3 RCT; FDA-approved 2024 for advanced-stage cHL; superior to BV-AVD in head-to-head comparison; practice-changing results

survival_benefit.rationale: 2-year PFS 92% vs 83% for BV-AVD; new standard for advanced-stage HL; particularly benefits young patients where long-term toxicity minimization is critical

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for Stage III/IV cHL 2024; nivolumab widely available; higher cost; early-stage investigation ongoing; not yet standard for Stage IIA favorable

safety_profile.rationale: No bleomycin (no pulmonary toxicity); less peripheral neuropathy than BV-AVD; immune-related adverse events (thyroiditis, hepatitis); endocrinopathy monitoring required

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-1 blockade exploits 9p24.1 amplification universal in classical HL leading to PD-L1/PD-L2 overexpression; HL has highest response rate to checkpoint inhibitors of any solid tumor or lymphoma

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab blocks PD-1 receptor on T cells, restoring anti-tumor immune response against Reed-Sternberg cells that constitutively overexpress PD-L1/PD-L2 due to 9p24.1 amplification. Combined with doxorubicin, vinblastine, and dacarbazine (AVD). HL is uniquely sensitive to checkpoint blockade due to genetic PD-L1 amplification.

treatments.name: PET-Adapted ABVD Alone (RT Omission Strategy)

evidence_level.rationale: RAPID trial, GHSG HD16, and multiple Phase 3 trials validate PET-adapted approach; NCCN Category 2A recommendation; avoids long-term radiation risks

survival_benefit.rationale: RAPID trial: PET-negative 3-year PFS 90.8% without RT vs 94.6% with RT; OS 99.0% vs 97.1% (numerically better without RT); small PFS trade-off for RT avoidance

accessibility.rationale: ABVD universally available; PET-CT widely accessible; eliminates need for radiation oncology referral; reduces total treatment duration and visits

safety_profile.rationale: Eliminates radiation-associated late effects (secondary malignancies, cardiovascular disease, thyroid dysfunction); preserves fertility maximally; ideal for 45-year-old with decades of life ahead

biomarker_match.rationale: PET response (Deauville score 1-2) is the key biomarker; identifies patients who achieve deep CR and can safely omit RT; applies across all HL subtypes

treatments.mechanism_of_action: PET-CT after 2-3 cycles of ABVD identifies patients achieving metabolic complete response (Deauville 1-2). PET-negative patients receive 1-2 additional ABVD cycles without consolidation RT, sparing long-term radiation toxicity while maintaining excellent cure rates. PET-positive patients proceed to standard combined modality therapy.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / Leukemia

treatments.name: BV + Nivolumab (Radiation-Free Early-Stage HL)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 trial; 154 patients; 92% CR rate (95% in favorable subgroup); chemotherapy-free and radiation-free approach; ASH 2024 presentation

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 96%, CR 92%; 2-year PFS 97%; 2-year DOR 96%; eliminates both chemotherapy and radiation toxicities while maintaining excellent outcomes

accessibility.rationale: Both agents FDA approved individually; combination not yet FDA-approved for frontline early-stage; clinical trial or off-label use; higher drug cost than ABVD

safety_profile.rationale: No chemotherapy toxicity (no myelosuppression, no cardiotoxicity, no gonadotoxicity); BV neuropathy and nivolumab immune-related AEs are main risks; avoids bleomycin pulmonary toxicity entirely

biomarker_match.rationale: CD30-targeted BV + PD-1 blockade is ideal for classical HL with universal CD30 expression and 9p24.1-driven PD-L1 overexpression; biologically rational combination

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Brentuximab vedotin (CD30-directed ADC) directly kills Reed-Sternberg cells while nivolumab (anti-PD-1) restores T-cell immunity against PD-L1-overexpressing tumor cells. This chemotherapy-free combination exploits both the CD30 and PD-L1 hallmarks of classical HL, potentially eliminating need for cytotoxic chemotherapy and radiation.

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (Anti-PD-1 Monotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-087 Phase 2 pivotal trial; FDA approved for R/R cHL after 3+ lines; ORR 72%, CR 28%; extensive real-world experience

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 72%, CR 28% in R/R setting; median DOR 16.5 months; reserved for relapsed/refractory disease; not standard for frontline Stage IIA

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for R/R cHL; widely available; IV every 3 weeks; well-established in oncology practice; high cost but insured in most settings

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated checkpoint inhibitor; immune-related AEs manageable (thyroiditis, hepatitis, colitis); no myelosuppression or alopecia; preserves fertility

biomarker_match.rationale: Classical HL has highest PD-L1 expression of any malignancy due to 9p24.1 amplification; HL is the poster child for checkpoint immunotherapy; no biomarker testing required

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab blocks PD-1 on T cells, releasing the immune checkpoint brake that Reed-Sternberg cells exploit through constitutive PD-L1/PD-L2 overexpression (9p24.1 amplification). Restores anti-tumor T-cell immunity in HL's inflammatory microenvironment.

treatments.name: Nivolumab (Anti-PD-1 Monotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: CheckMate 205/039 pivotal trials; FDA approved for R/R cHL after ASCT and BV; 243 patients in pivotal study; extensive follow-up

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 69%, CR 16%; median DOR 22.2 months for CR patients; reserved for relapsed/refractory; not frontline for Stage IIA

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for R/R cHL; widely available; IV every 2 weeks; part of standard salvage paradigm

safety_profile.rationale: Manageable immune-related AEs; no myelosuppression; preserves fertility; well-characterized safety profile from extensive use across oncology

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-1 blockade ideal for cHL due to universal PD-L1 overexpression from 9p24.1 amplification; nodular sclerosis particularly inflammatory microenvironment enhances checkpoint blockade response

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab blocks PD-1 checkpoint on T cells, restoring anti-tumor immunity against Reed-Sternberg cells that overexpress PD-L1/PD-L2 due to chromosome 9p24.1 amplification. This genetic feature makes classical HL uniquely responsive to PD-1 blockade.

key_evidence.journal: New England Journal of Medicine / JCO

treatments.name: BrECADD (Brentuximab Vedotin + Etoposide, Cyclophosphamide, Doxorubicin, Dacarbazine, Dexamethasone)

evidence_level.rationale: GHSG HD21 Phase 3 RCT with 1500 patients; non-inferior to eBEACOPP with better tolerability; European approval 2025 for Stage IIb+/III/IV

survival_benefit.rationale: Non-inferior PFS to eBEACOPP in advanced-stage; PET-adapted de-escalation to 2 cycles possible; designed for advanced/unfavorable disease, not primary option for Stage IIA favorable

accessibility.rationale: European approval 2025 for advanced-stage; not yet FDA approved in US; available at GHSG centers and some international sites; more complex regimen than ABVD

safety_profile.rationale: 42% treatment-related morbidity vs 59% for eBEACOPP; no bleomycin or procarbazine; reduced gonadotoxicity vs BEACOPP; peripheral neuropathy from BV

biomarker_match.rationale: CD30-targeted BV component provides molecular precision; effective across all classical HL subtypes; particularly relevant for unfavorable risk features

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Brentuximab vedotin (CD30-directed ADC) combined with etoposide, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, dacarbazine, and dexamethasone. Replaces bleomycin and procarbazine from BEACOPP with BV, maintaining efficacy while reducing gonadotoxicity and pulmonary toxicity.
treatments.availability: EMA Approved (2025) / Not yet FDA Approved

treatments.name: Camidanlumab Tesirine (Anti-CD25 ADC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 ADCT-301-201 study published 2025; ORR 70%, CR 33% in heavily pretreated R/R cHL; FDA advised against approval filing; safety concerns

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 70.1%, CRR 33.3% in R/R setting after BV and PD-1 inhibitor failure; addresses unmet need in multiply relapsed HL; limited long-term survival data

accessibility.rationale: Not FDA approved; FDA advised against filing; available only through clinical trials or compassionate use; regulatory path uncertain

safety_profile.rationale: Significant safety concerns: Guillain-Barre syndrome, polyradiculopathy, skin toxicities; immune-related neurologic AEs dose-limiting; FDA safety concerns drove advisory against filing

biomarker_match.rationale: CD25 expressed on Reed-Sternberg cells and regulatory T cells in HL microenvironment; dual targeting of tumor and immunosuppressive cells; novel mechanism addressing BV/CPI-resistant disease

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-CD25 antibody conjugated to pyrrolobenzodiazepine (PBD) dimer toxin. Targets CD25 (IL-2 receptor alpha) expressed on Reed-Sternberg cells and regulatory T cells. Delivers SG3199 DNA crosslinker to kill tumor cells and deplete immunosuppressive Tregs in HL microenvironment.

treatments.name: Autologous Stem Cell Transplant (ASCT) + BV/CPI Salvage

evidence_level.rationale: Standard of care for relapsed/refractory HL; multiple Phase 3 trials validate ASCT approach; PD-1 inhibitor and BV combinations improve pre-transplant responses

survival_benefit.rationale: 50-60% long-term cure rate with ASCT for chemo-sensitive relapse; BV + nivolumab salvage achieves 80%+ CR before ASCT; reserved for relapsed disease

accessibility.rationale: ASCT available at major transplant centers; requires adequate organ function and performance status; 45-year-old is appropriate candidate; moderate cost

safety_profile.rationale: High-dose conditioning toxicity (BEAM/LEAM); transplant-related mortality 1-3%; prolonged cytopenias; infection risk during engraftment; long-term secondary malignancy risk

biomarker_match.rationale: Applicable to all classical HL subtypes in relapse; BV maintenance post-ASCT extends PFS; PD-1 inhibitors as bridge to transplant exploit HL biology

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Salvage chemotherapy (with BV and/or PD-1 inhibitor) achieves deep remission, followed by high-dose conditioning chemotherapy and autologous stem cell rescue. ASCT provides intensive therapy beyond conventional chemotherapy limits, with BV maintenance consolidation post-transplant.

clinical_trials.title: Testing Standard of Care in Early-Stage Hodgkin Lymphoma (Collaborative Trial)
clinical_trials.early_results: Ongoing enrollment; aims to define optimal frontline approach for early-stage disease

clinical_trials.title: Brentuximab Vedotin and Nivolumab in Radiation-Free Treatment of Early-Stage Classic Hodgkin Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: CR 92% overall (95% favorable); 2-year PFS 97%; chemotherapy-free and radiation-free approach

clinical_trials.title: Nivolumab + AVD vs BV-AVD in Advanced Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma (Long-Term Follow-Up)
clinical_trials.early_results: 2-year PFS 92% (N-AVD) vs 83% (BV-AVD); FDA approved 2024; paradigm-changing results

clinical_trials.title: BrECADD vs eBEACOPP in Advanced-Stage Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: BrECADD non-inferior to eBEACOPP; significantly lower treatment-related morbidity (42% vs 59%); EC approval 2025

clinical_trials.title: Camidanlumab Tesirine (Anti-CD25 ADC) in R/R Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma
biomarker_requirements: R/R after BV and PD-1 inhibitor
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 70.1%, CR 33.3%; safety concerns led FDA to advise against approval filing

clinical_trials.title: BV-AVD for First-Line Treatment of Early-Stage Unfavorable Hodgkin Lymphoma
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 96%, CR 92%; 2-year PFS 97%; supports BV-AVD in early-stage unfavorable disease

combination_strategies.rationale: International standard for early-stage favorable HL; 5-year OS 96.6%; minimal toxicity with only 2 chemotherapy cycles and reduced-dose RT; >90% cure rate
Chemotherapy-free and radiation-free combination exploits both CD30 and PD-L1 hallmarks of cHL; CR 92%; preserves fertility completely; ideal for 45-year-old male with decades of survivorship ahead
Superior PFS to BV-AVD in head-to-head Phase 3; FDA approved 2024; PD-1 blockade synergizes with chemotherapy-induced immunogenic cell death; eliminates bleomycin pulmonary toxicity
BrECADD replaces toxic BEACOPP components with BV; non-inferior efficacy with significantly reduced morbidity; EC approved 2025; preserves fertility better than BEACOPP
For relapsed HL: PD-1 + BV achieves 80%+ CR as bridge to ASCT; AETHERA trial showed BV maintenance post-ASCT extends PFS; curative intent in 50-60% of relapsed patients

supportive_care.evidence: ASCO Fertility Preservation Guidelines; ABVD causes transient azoospermia in one-third of males recovering in 6-18 months; BV-AVD and N-AVD have similar profiles; sperm banking success rates 33-56% pregnancy rate
supportive_care.benefit: Preserves reproductive options for 45-year-old male; ABVD has lowest gonadotoxicity but sperm banking still recommended before any HL treatment; simple, rapid procedure; should be completed before first cycle

supportive_care.evidence: RAPID trial, GHSG HD16, NCCN guidelines; Deauville criteria-based assessment after 2 cycles guides therapy modification including RT omission
supportive_care.benefit: Identifies patients who can safely omit RT (PET-negative: Deauville 1-2); enables treatment de-escalation in early responders; identifies early failures for treatment intensification; standard of care in all HL protocols

supportive_care.evidence: Bleomycin pulmonary toxicity occurs in 2-10% of patients; risk increases with age, cumulative dose, renal impairment, and G-CSF use; DLCO monitoring recommended before each cycle
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of bleomycin-induced pneumonitis; DLCO decline >20% from baseline triggers bleomycin discontinuation; particularly relevant if ABVD chosen (BV-AVD eliminates this risk)

supportive_care.evidence: Doxorubicin present in ABVD, BV-AVD, and N-AVD; cumulative dose monitoring; baseline LVEF assessment before treatment; 2 cycles of ABVD delivers low cumulative anthracycline dose
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of anthracycline-induced cardiomyopathy; limited risk with 2 cycles but important for 45-year-old with long survivorship expected; mediastinal RT adds cardiovascular risk if needed

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Distress Management Guidelines; Hodgkin lymphoma survivors face unique long-term health surveillance needs; young adult survivorship programs
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses treatment anxiety (despite high cure rate); establishes long-term surveillance plan for secondary malignancies, cardiovascular disease, and thyroid dysfunction; connects with age-appropriate support services

supportive_care.evidence: Cervical ISRT field includes thyroid gland; hypothyroidism develops in 20-50% of patients receiving neck radiation; onset typically 2-5 years post-treatment
supportive_care.benefit: Annual TSH monitoring detects subclinical hypothyroidism before symptomatic disease; thyroid hormone replacement simple and effective; particularly important with cervical Stage IIA where thyroid is in radiation field
